metadata.name: Kotlin
metadata.description: Platforms > Android deep-dive. KotlinJsonUI + kjui CLI + Dynamic mode. Four sections: What you get / Dual-mode output (Compose + XML Views) / Dynamic mode hot reload / Production caveats. ~10-min read.

methods.name: onAppear
methods.description: Seed nextReadLinks from the module-scope NEXT_READ_ENTRIES catalog (two rows: next_swift -> /platforms/swift, next_react -> /platforms/react) and stamp currentLanguage from StringManager.language. Each row's titleKey / descriptionKey is resolved through StringManager with the platforms_kotlin_ namespace prefix.

methods.name: onNavigate
methods.description: Client-side navigation via router.push(url). Destinations are the spec-mapped URLs enumerated in transitions: /, /platforms/swift, /platforms/react.

vars.name: nextReadLinks
vars.description: Two closing 'read next' cards pointing at /platforms/swift and /platforms/react. Seeded by onAppear from the NEXT_READ_ENTRIES static catalog and re-seeded by onToggleLanguage.

notes: Second live Platforms entry. Flip PLATFORMS_ENTRIES row 2 (kotlin) to 'live'.
Audience: Android engineers evaluating JsonUI vs staying on hand-written Compose. Copy emphasises dual-mode (Compose + XML for legacy codebases) and the Dynamic mode hot-reload parity with iOS.
